SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.automation

Class ProcessAutomation

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.automation.ProcessAutomation
public class ProcessAutomation extends Object
Provides a stable, string-addressable automation API for interacting with a running NeqSim ProcessSystem or ProcessModel. Variables in the simulation are reachable through stable dot-notation paths such as "separator-1.gasOutStream.temperature", removing the need to navigate Java objects directly.

When backed by a ProcessModel (multi-area plant), addresses use area-qualified syntax: "AreaName::UnitName.property" or "AreaName::UnitName.port.property".

The core operations are:

  • getUnitList() — list all unit operation names
  • getAreaList() — list all process area names (ProcessModel only)
  • getUnitList(String) — list units in a specific area
  • getVariableList(String) — list all variables for a unit
  • getVariableValue(String, String) — read a variable by address
  • setVariableValue(String, double, String) — write an input variable

Address format: unitName.property or unitName.streamPort.property

Example usage: 

 // Single ProcessSystem
 ProcessAutomation auto = new ProcessAutomation(process);

 // List all units
 List<String> units = auto.getUnitList();

 // List variables for a separator
 List<SimulationVariable> vars = auto.getVariableList("HP Sep");

 // Read a value
 double temp = auto.getVariableValue("HP Sep.gasOutStream.temperature", "C");

 // Set an input
 auto.setVariableValue("Compressor.outletPressure", 120.0, "bara");

 // Multi-area ProcessModel
 ProcessAutomation plantAuto = new ProcessAutomation(plantModel);
 List<String> areas = plantAuto.getAreaList();
 List<String> areaUnits = plantAuto.getUnitList("Separation");
 double p = plantAuto.getVariableValue("Separation::HP Sep.pressure", "bara");
Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa

    Class
    Description

    ProcessAutomation.ResolvedUnit
    Holds a resolved unit and its address prefix for variable naming.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ProcessAutomation(ProcessModel processModel)
    Creates an automation facade for a multi-area process model.
    ProcessAutomation(ProcessSystem processSystem)
    Creates an automation facade for a single process system.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    private void
    addOutletStreamVariables(List<SimulationVariable> vars, String unitName, ProcessEquipmentInterface unit)
    Adds outlet stream variables for single-outlet equipment.
    private void
    addStreamOutputVariables(List<SimulationVariable> vars, String prefix, StreamInterface stream)
    Adds read-only stream variables for an output stream.
    private void
    addStreamVariables(List<SimulationVariable> vars, String prefix, StreamInterface stream, boolean isInput)
    Adds stream variables (temperature, pressure, flowRate) with appropriate INPUT/OUTPUT type.
    private String
    buildAutoCorrectedJson(String originalAddress, String correctedAddress, double value, String unit, AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult diag)
    Builds a JSON response for a successful auto-corrected get operation.
    private String
    buildAutoCorrectedSetJson(String originalAddress, String correctedAddress, double value, String unit, AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult diag)
    Builds a JSON response for a successful auto-corrected set operation.
    private String
    buildSetSuccessJson(String address, double value, String unit, String warningJson)
    Builds a success JSON response for a set operation.
    private String
    buildSuccessJson(String address, double value, String unit)
    Builds a success JSON response for a get operation.
    private List<SimulationVariable>
    buildVariableList(String unitName, ProcessEquipmentInterface unit)
    Builds the list of variables exposed by a unit operation.
    private com.google.gson.JsonObject
    compositionJson(SystemInterface fluid, boolean mass)
    Builds a {component → fraction} JSON object for a fluid.
    String
    describe()
    Returns a single JSON manifest describing the entire flowsheet schema: areas (if any), units with type and variables, and stable schema version.
    private AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult
    diagnoseAndAttemptRecovery(String address, IllegalArgumentException error)
    Diagnoses an address resolution failure and attempts recovery via fuzzy matching.
    private static String
    emptyToNull(String s)
    Returns null for a null or empty string, otherwise the original string.
    private List<SimulationVariable>
    enrichVariableMetadata(List<SimulationVariable> variables)
    Enriches sparse variable descriptors with metadata useful to agents.
    private SimulationVariable
    enrichVariableMetadata(SimulationVariable variable)
    Enriches a single variable descriptor.
    String
    evaluate(Map<String,Double> setpoints, String unitOfMeasure, List<String> readbacks)
    Convenience overload of evaluate(Map, String, List, String, int, double) that uses the same unit for setpoints and read-backs and sensible default convergence settings (30 iterations, relative tolerance 5e-3, which is robust for plants with near-zero-flow anti-surge recycles).
    String
    evaluate(Map<String,Double> setpoints, String setpointUnit, List<String> readbacks, String readbackUnit, int maxIterations, double tolerance)
    Atomic evaluate step for closed-loop agentic optimization: applies a batch of decision variables, runs the process until convergence, gates the result on the run status, and reads back the requested objective / constraint variables — all returned as a single JSON document.
    private String
    extractPropertyName(String address)
    Extracts the property name from an address (the last dot-separated segment).
    private String
    extractPropertyPath(String address)
    Returns the property path that the typed validators see (the portion of the address after the unit name).
    private ProcessEquipmentInterface
    findByReferenceDesignation(String areaName, String refDesString)
    Finds a unit by its IEC 81346 reference designation string.
    private ProcessEquipmentInterface
    findUnit(String areaName, String unitName)
    Finds a unit by name, optionally scoped to a specific area.
    List<AdjustableParameter>
    getAdjustableParameters()
    Returns the registry of adjustable parameters (degrees of freedom) for the underlying process.
    String
    getAdjustableParametersJson()
    Returns the registry of adjustable parameters as a JSON string.
    List<String>
    getAllowedUnits(String address)
    Returns a list of unit-of-measure strings that are valid for a given address.
    List<String>
    getAreaList()
    Returns the names of all process areas.

    getDiagnostics()
    Returns the diagnostics instance for this automation facade.

    getEquipmentProperty(ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String property, String uom)

    getEquipmentType(String unitName)

    getNeighbors(String unitName)
    Returns the upstream and downstream neighbors of a unit, derived from its inlet and outlet streams.

    getPlainUnitNames(ProcessSystem ps)

    getRunStatusJson()
    Returns the structured outcome of the most recent run as a JSON string.
    String
    getSchemaVersion()
    Returns the stable schema version of JSON responses produced by this facade.

    getStreamProperty(StreamInterface stream, String property, String uom)

    getStructured(String address)
    Returns a structured JSON element for a variable address.
    String
    getTopology()
    Returns a JSON description of the flowsheet topology: equipment with their declared inlet and outlet streams, and explicit ProcessConnection edges when available.
    List<String>
    getUnitList()
    Returns the names of all unit operations.
    List<String>
    getUnitList(String areaName)
    Returns the names of unit operations in a specific process area.
    String
    getUtilizationSnapshot()
    Returns a stable, side-effect-free JSON utilization snapshot of every unit in the flowsheet.

    getValues(List<String> addresses, String unitOfMeasure)
    Reads many variables in a single call.

    Returns all available variables for the named unit operation.

    getVariableList(String unitName, SimulationVariable.VariableType type)
    Returns variables for the named unit, filtered by type.
    double
    getVariableValue(String address, String unitOfMeasure)
    Reads the current value of a simulation variable.
    String
    getVariableValueSafe(String address, String unitOfMeasure)
    Reads a variable value with self-healing: if the exact address fails, attempts auto-correction via fuzzy matching against known unit names and variable addresses.
    WriteValidatorRegistry
    getWriteValidatorRegistry()
    Returns the registry of typed write validators consulted by setVariableValueValidated(String, double, String) and setValuesTransactional(Map, String).

    inferApplicability(SimulationVariable variable)
    Infers an applicability note for a variable.
    private String
    inferVariableCategory(SimulationVariable variable)
    Infers a variable category from its address and property name.
    boolean
    isDirty()
    Returns whether any input has been set via setVariableValue(java.lang.String, double, java.lang.String) since the last successful run() or runIfDirty().
    boolean
    isMultiArea()
    Returns whether this automation facade is backed by a ProcessModel (multi-area).
    void
    markDirty()
    Manually marks this facade as dirty, e.g. after modifying the underlying process system directly through other Java APIs.
    private void
    mergeRunStatus(com.google.gson.JsonObject root)
    Merges the most recent RunStatus fields (success, failedUnitName, failedUnitError) into the supplied JSON object.
    AgenticProcessOptimizer
    newOptimizer()
    Creates a new AgenticProcessOptimizer bound to this automation facade.
    private List<String>
    resolveScope(String scope)
    Resolves a snapshot scope string to a list of (possibly area-qualified) unit names.
    private StreamInterface
    resolveStreamPort(ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String portName)
    Resolves a stream port name to the actual stream object.
    private ProcessAutomation.ResolvedUnit
    resolveUnit(String unitName)
    Resolves a unit name (optionally area-qualified) to the equipment and its address prefix.
    private void
    rollbackSnapshot(List<TransactionalBatchResult.WriteOutcome> snapshot, String unitOfMeasure)
    Restores values from a snapshot stored in TransactionalBatchResult.WriteOutcome entries and re-runs the simulation to leave it in a coherent state.
    void
    run()
    Runs the underlying ProcessSystem or ProcessModel and clears the dirty flag on success.
    boolean
    runIfDirty()
    Runs the underlying process only if isDirty() is true.
    String
    runJson()
    Runs the underlying process and returns the structured RunStatus as JSON, never throwing on a solver failure.
    String
    runUntilConvergedJson(int maxIterations, double tolerance)
    Runs the process until convergence (or an iteration limit) and returns a unified JSON report suitable for per-trial feasibility gating in agentic optimization.
    private static Double
    sanitizeBound(double value)
    Converts an adjuster bound to a nullable Double, mapping sentinel "unbounded" values (magnitude at or beyond UNBOUNDED_THRESHOLD) and non-finite values to null.
    private ProcessEquipmentInterface
    searchByRefDes(ProcessSystem ps, String refDesString)
    Searches a process system for equipment matching a reference designation string.
    private void
    setEquipmentProperty(ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String property, double value, String uom)
    Sets a property value directly on an equipment object.
    private void
    setStreamProperty(StreamInterface stream, String property, double value, String uom)
    Sets a property value on a stream.
    int
    setValues(Map<String,Double> updates, String unitOfMeasure, boolean runAfter)
    Sets many input variables in a single call.
    TransactionalBatchResult
    setValuesTransactional(Map<String,Double> updates, String unitOfMeasure)
    Applies many input writes atomically: validates every write up-front, snapshots the current values, applies the writes, runs the simulation, and rolls back to the snapshot when any phase fails.
    void
    setVariableValue(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
    Sets the value of a simulation input variable.
    void
    setVariableValueAndRun(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
    Sets an input variable then immediately runs the process.
    String
    setVariableValueSafe(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
    Sets a variable value with self-healing: if the exact address fails, attempts auto-correction via fuzzy matching.
    WriteValidationResult
    setVariableValueValidated(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
    Like setVariableValue(String, double, String) but first runs the WriteValidatorRegistry against the proposed write.
    void
    setWriteValidatorRegistry(WriteValidatorRegistry registry)
    Replaces the registry of typed write validators.
    String
    snapshot(String scope)
    Returns a JSON snapshot of all variables for a unit, an area, or the entire process.
    private String
    stripUnitPrefix(String address, String unitAddr)
    Removes a unit-name prefix from a variable address, leaving the property path.
    private Double
    tryReadValue(String address, String unitOfMeasure)
    Reads the current value of an address without throwing.
    private ProcessEquipmentInterface
    tryResolveEquipment(String address)
    Resolves the ProcessEquipmentInterface addressed by an automation address (handling the optional area prefix and stripping any port/property suffix).
    AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult
    validateAddress(String address)
    Validates an address without throwing.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Field Details

    • AREA_SEPARATOR

      public static final String AREA_SEPARATOR
      Separator between area name and unit address in multi-area mode.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • SCHEMA_VERSION

      public static final String SCHEMA_VERSION
      Stable schema version for JSON responses produced by this facade. Increment the minor for additive changes, the major for breaking changes. Agents and MCP clients should branch on this value when parsing responses.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • processSystem

      private final ProcessSystem processSystem

    • processModel

      private final ProcessModel processModel

    • diagnostics

      private final AutomationDiagnostics diagnostics

    • validatorRegistry

      private WriteValidatorRegistry validatorRegistry
      Registry of typed write validators consulted by setVariableValueValidated(String, double, String) and setValuesTransactional(Map, String). Defaults to WriteValidatorRegistry.createDefault().

    • dirty

      private boolean dirty
      Dirty flag: true when one or more inputs have been changed via setVariableValue(java.lang.String, double, java.lang.String) since the last successful run. Used by isDirty(), runIfDirty(), and the stale warning emitted by getVariableValueSafe(java.lang.String, java.lang.String).

    • UNBOUNDED_THRESHOLD

      private static final double UNBOUNDED_THRESHOLD
      Sentinel threshold above which an adjuster bound is treated as unbounded. Adjusters default to +/-1e10, so any magnitude at or beyond 1e9 is reported as null (no bound).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • ProcessAutomation

      public ProcessAutomation(ProcessSystem processSystem)
      Creates an automation facade for a single process system.
      Parameters:
      processSystem - the process system to automate

    • ProcessAutomation

      public ProcessAutomation(ProcessModel processModel)
      Creates an automation facade for a multi-area process model.

      In this mode, addresses use area-qualified syntax: "AreaName::UnitName.property".

      Parameters:
      processModel - the process model to automate

  • Method Details

    • isMultiArea

      public boolean isMultiArea()
      Returns whether this automation facade is backed by a ProcessModel (multi-area).
      Returns:
      true if backed by a ProcessModel, false if backed by a single ProcessSystem

    • getDiagnostics

      public AutomationDiagnostics getDiagnostics()
      Returns the diagnostics instance for this automation facade. The diagnostics provide fuzzy name matching, auto-correction, physical value validation, and operation history tracking.
      Returns:
      the automation diagnostics

    • getVariableValueSafe

      public String getVariableValueSafe(String address, String unitOfMeasure)
      Reads a variable value with self-healing: if the exact address fails, attempts auto-correction via fuzzy matching against known unit names and variable addresses. Returns a JSON string with the value on success, or a diagnostic result with suggestions on failure.
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address, e.g. "separator-1.gasOutStream.temperature"
      unitOfMeasure - the desired unit
      Returns:
      JSON result string with either value or diagnostic information

    • setVariableValueSafe

      public String setVariableValueSafe(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
      Sets a variable value with self-healing: if the exact address fails, attempts auto-correction via fuzzy matching. Also validates the value against physical bounds before setting.
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      value - the value to set
      unitOfMeasure - the unit of the value
      Returns:
      JSON result string with either success or diagnostic information

    • getAreaList

      public List<String> getAreaList()
      Returns the names of all process areas. Only available when backed by a ProcessModel.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of area names in insertion order
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if backed by a single ProcessSystem

    • getUnitList

      public List<String> getUnitList()
      Returns the names of all unit operations. When backed by a ProcessModel, returns area-qualified names in the format "AreaName::UnitName".
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of unit operation names

    • getUnitList

      public List<String> getUnitList(String areaName)
      Returns the names of unit operations in a specific process area. Only available when backed by a ProcessModel.
      Parameters:
      areaName - the name of the process area
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of unit operation names (without area prefix)
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if backed by a single ProcessSystem
      IllegalArgumentException - if the area is not found

    • getVariableList

      public List<SimulationVariable> getVariableList(String unitName)
      Returns all available variables for the named unit operation.
      Parameters:
      unitName - the name of the unit operation
      Returns:
      list of variable descriptors
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the unit is not found

    • getVariableList

      public List<SimulationVariable> getVariableList(String unitName, SimulationVariable.VariableType type)
      Returns variables for the named unit, filtered by type.

      When backed by a ProcessModel, the unitName may be area-qualified: "AreaName::UnitName".

      Parameters:
      unitName - the name of the unit operation, optionally area-qualified
      type - the variable type filter, or null for all variables
      Returns:
      list of variable descriptors matching the filter
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the unit is not found

    • getAdjustableParameters

      public List<AdjustableParameter> getAdjustableParameters()
      Returns the registry of adjustable parameters (degrees of freedom) for the underlying process.

      The registry combines two sources:

      • Writable INPUT variables — every SimulationVariable of type SimulationVariable.VariableType.INPUT that is writable, with its existing bounds and default unit.
      • Adjuster unit operations — each Adjuster is reported with the unit operation and property it actually drives (AdjustableParameter.getTargetUnitName() and AdjustableParameter.getTargetProperty()). This removes the ambiguity that arises when an adjuster's name does not match the variable it controls.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of adjustable parameter descriptors

    • getAdjustableParametersJson

      public String getAdjustableParametersJson()
      Returns the registry of adjustable parameters as a JSON string.
      Returns:
      JSON string {schemaVersion, count, parameters:[{name, address, unit, lowerBound, upperBound, targetUnitName, targetProperty, source}]}

    • newOptimizer

      public AgenticProcessOptimizer newOptimizer()
      Creates a new AgenticProcessOptimizer bound to this automation facade. This is the recommended entry point for closed-loop, ML- and agent-driven optimization over the underlying process: the optimizer drives evaluate(Map, String, java.util.List) for every trial, speaks schema-versioned JSON, and never throws.
      Returns:
      a fresh optimizer wrapping this facade

    • sanitizeBound

      private static Double sanitizeBound(double value)
      Converts an adjuster bound to a nullable Double, mapping sentinel "unbounded" values (magnitude at or beyond UNBOUNDED_THRESHOLD) and non-finite values to null.
      Parameters:
      value - the raw bound value
      Returns:
      the bound, or null if effectively unbounded

    • emptyToNull

      private static String emptyToNull(String s)
      Returns null for a null or empty string, otherwise the original string.
      Parameters:
      s - the string to check
      Returns:
      null if empty, otherwise s

    • getEquipmentType

      public String getEquipmentType(String unitName)
      Returns the simple class name (equipment type) of a unit operation. Useful for discovering what kind of equipment a unit is, e.g. "Compressor", "Separator", "PipeBeggsAndBrills".
      Parameters:
      unitName - the name of the unit operation, optionally area-qualified
      Returns:
      the simple class name of the equipment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the unit is not found

    • getVariableValue

      public double getVariableValue(String address, String unitOfMeasure)
      Reads the current value of a simulation variable.

      When backed by a ProcessModel, the address must be area-qualified: "AreaName::unitName.property" or "AreaName::unitName.streamPort.property".

      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address, e.g. "separator-1.gasOutStream.temperature" or "Separation::separator-1.gasOutStream.temperature"
      unitOfMeasure - the desired unit, e.g. "C", "bara", "kg/hr". Pass null or empty for default units
      Returns:
      the variable value in the requested unit
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the address cannot be resolved

    • setVariableValue

      public void setVariableValue(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
      Sets the value of a simulation input variable. Only variables with INPUT type can be set.

      When backed by a ProcessModel, the address must be area-qualified: "AreaName::Compressor.outletPressure".

      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address, e.g. "Compressor.outletPressure" or "Compression::Compressor.outletPressure"
      value - the value to set
      unitOfMeasure - the unit of the provided value, e.g. "bara", "C". Pass null or empty for default units
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the address cannot be resolved or the variable is read-only

    • resolveUnit

      private ProcessAutomation.ResolvedUnit resolveUnit(String unitName)
      Resolves a unit name (optionally area-qualified) to the equipment and its address prefix.
      Parameters:
      unitName - the unit name, optionally with area prefix "AreaName::UnitName"
      Returns:
      the resolved unit and address prefix
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the unit is not found

    • findUnit

      private ProcessEquipmentInterface findUnit(String areaName, String unitName)
      Finds a unit by name, optionally scoped to a specific area.
      Parameters:
      areaName - the area name, or null to search the single ProcessSystem (or all areas)
      unitName - the unit name
      Returns:
      the equipment
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the unit is not found

    • getPlainUnitNames

      private List<String> getPlainUnitNames(ProcessSystem ps)
      Gets plain (non-area-qualified) unit names from a ProcessSystem.
      Parameters:
      ps - the process system
      Returns:
      list of unit names

    • findByReferenceDesignation

      private ProcessEquipmentInterface findByReferenceDesignation(String areaName, String refDesString)
      Finds a unit by its IEC 81346 reference designation string.

      Searches all equipment in the relevant process system(s) for a matching reference designation. This enables addressing equipment by their IEC 81346 codes, e.g. "=A1-B1" or "-K2".

      Parameters:
      areaName - the area name to search within (null to search all)
      refDesString - the reference designation string to match
      Returns:
      the matching equipment, or null if not found

    • searchByRefDes

      private ProcessEquipmentInterface searchByRefDes(ProcessSystem ps, String refDesString)
      Searches a process system for equipment matching a reference designation string.
      Parameters:
      ps - the process system to search
      refDesString - the reference designation to match
      Returns:
      the matching equipment, or null if not found

    • buildVariableList

      private List<SimulationVariable> buildVariableList(String unitName, ProcessEquipmentInterface unit)
      Builds the list of variables exposed by a unit operation.
      Parameters:
      unitName - the unit name (used as address prefix)
      unit - the unit operation
      Returns:
      list of variables

    • enrichVariableMetadata

      private List<SimulationVariable> enrichVariableMetadata(List<SimulationVariable> variables)
      Enriches sparse variable descriptors with metadata useful to agents.
      Parameters:
      variables - raw variables discovered from equipment
      Returns:
      enriched variable descriptors

    • enrichVariableMetadata

      private SimulationVariable enrichVariableMetadata(SimulationVariable variable)
      Enriches a single variable descriptor.
      Parameters:
      variable - raw variable descriptor
      Returns:
      enriched variable descriptor

    • inferVariableCategory

      private String inferVariableCategory(SimulationVariable variable)
      Infers a variable category from its address and property name.
      Parameters:
      variable - variable descriptor
      Returns:
      category string

    • inferApplicability

      private String inferApplicability(SimulationVariable variable)
      Infers an applicability note for a variable.
      Parameters:
      variable - variable descriptor
      Returns:
      applicability note

    • addStreamVariables

      private void addStreamVariables(List<SimulationVariable> vars, String prefix, StreamInterface stream, boolean isInput)
      Adds stream variables (temperature, pressure, flowRate) with appropriate INPUT/OUTPUT type.
      Parameters:
      vars - the list to add to
      prefix - the address prefix
      stream - the stream
      isInput - whether the stream properties are settable

    • addStreamOutputVariables

      private void addStreamOutputVariables(List<SimulationVariable> vars, String prefix, StreamInterface stream)
      Adds read-only stream variables for an output stream.
      Parameters:
      vars - the list to add to
      prefix - the address prefix including port name
      stream - the stream

    • addOutletStreamVariables

      private void addOutletStreamVariables(List<SimulationVariable> vars, String unitName, ProcessEquipmentInterface unit)
      Adds outlet stream variables for single-outlet equipment.
      Parameters:
      vars - the list to add to
      unitName - the unit name
      unit - the equipment

    • getEquipmentProperty

      private double getEquipmentProperty(ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String property, String uom)
      Gets a property value directly from an equipment object.
      Parameters:
      unit - the equipment
      property - the property name
      uom - the desired unit of measure
      Returns:
      the property value

    • setEquipmentProperty

      private void setEquipmentProperty(ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String property, double value, String uom)
      Sets a property value directly on an equipment object.
      Parameters:
      unit - the equipment
      property - the property name
      value - the value to set
      uom - the unit of measure for the value

    • resolveStreamPort

      private StreamInterface resolveStreamPort(ProcessEquipmentInterface unit, String portName)
      Resolves a stream port name to the actual stream object.
      Parameters:
      unit - the equipment
      portName - the port name, e.g. "gasOutStream", "liquidOutStream", "outletStream"
      Returns:
      the resolved stream, or null if not found

    • getStreamProperty

      private double getStreamProperty(StreamInterface stream, String property, String uom)
      Gets a property value from a stream.
      Parameters:
      stream - the stream
      property - the property name
      uom - the desired unit of measure
      Returns:
      the property value

    • setStreamProperty

      private void setStreamProperty(StreamInterface stream, String property, double value, String uom)
      Sets a property value on a stream.
      Parameters:
      stream - the stream
      property - the property name
      value - the value to set
      uom - the unit of measure

    • diagnoseAndAttemptRecovery

      private AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult diagnoseAndAttemptRecovery(String address, IllegalArgumentException error)
      Diagnoses an address resolution failure and attempts recovery via fuzzy matching.
      Parameters:
      address - the failed address
      error - the caught exception
      Returns:
      a diagnostic result with suggestions and possible auto-correction

    • extractPropertyName

      private String extractPropertyName(String address)
      Extracts the property name from an address (the last dot-separated segment).
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      Returns:
      the property name

    • buildSuccessJson

      private String buildSuccessJson(String address, double value, String unit)
      Builds a success JSON response for a get operation.
      Parameters:
      address - the address
      value - the value
      unit - the unit of measure
      Returns:
      JSON string

    • buildAutoCorrectedJson

      private String buildAutoCorrectedJson(String originalAddress, String correctedAddress, double value, String unit, AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult diag)
      Builds a JSON response for a successful auto-corrected get operation.
      Parameters:
      originalAddress - the original address that failed
      correctedAddress - the corrected address that succeeded
      value - the value read
      unit - the unit of measure
      diag - the diagnostic result
      Returns:
      JSON string

    • buildSetSuccessJson

      private String buildSetSuccessJson(String address, double value, String unit, String warningJson)
      Builds a success JSON response for a set operation.
      Parameters:
      address - the address
      value - the value set
      unit - the unit of measure
      warningJson - JSON warning from bounds validation, or null
      Returns:
      JSON string

    • buildAutoCorrectedSetJson

      private String buildAutoCorrectedSetJson(String originalAddress, String correctedAddress, double value, String unit, AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult diag)
      Builds a JSON response for a successful auto-corrected set operation.
      Parameters:
      originalAddress - the original address that failed
      correctedAddress - the corrected address that succeeded
      value - the value set
      unit - the unit of measure
      diag - the diagnostic result
      Returns:
      JSON string

    • getSchemaVersion

      public String getSchemaVersion()
      Returns the stable schema version of JSON responses produced by this facade. Agents and MCP clients can branch on this value when parsing responses (see SCHEMA_VERSION).
      Returns:
      the schema version string, e.g. "1.0"

    • isDirty

      public boolean isDirty()
      Returns whether any input has been set via setVariableValue(java.lang.String, double, java.lang.String) since the last successful run() or runIfDirty(). When true, outputs returned by getVariableValue(java.lang.String, java.lang.String) may be stale and should be refreshed by calling run().
      Returns:
      true if the underlying process needs to be re-run, false otherwise

    • markDirty

      public void markDirty()
      Manually marks this facade as dirty, e.g. after modifying the underlying process system directly through other Java APIs.

    • run

      public void run()
      Runs the underlying ProcessSystem or ProcessModel and clears the dirty flag on success.
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the underlying solver fails

    • runIfDirty

      public boolean runIfDirty()
      Runs the underlying process only if isDirty() is true. Returns whether a run was actually performed.
      Returns:
      true if a run was triggered, false if the process was already clean

    • setVariableValueAndRun

      public void setVariableValueAndRun(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
      Sets an input variable then immediately runs the process. Equivalent to setVariableValue(String, double, String) followed by run() but provided as a single atomic call for agents that want the result of one specific change.
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      value - the value to set
      unitOfMeasure - the unit of the provided value, or null for default units
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the address cannot be resolved
      RuntimeException - if the subsequent run fails

    • getRunStatusJson

      public String getRunStatusJson()
      Returns the structured outcome of the most recent run as a JSON string. Lets agents inspect whether the last run succeeded and, if not, which unit failed, without catching and parsing a RuntimeException.
      Returns:
      schema-versioned JSON describing the last run outcome (see RunStatus.toJson())

    • runJson

      public String runJson()
      Runs the underlying process and returns the structured RunStatus as JSON, never throwing on a solver failure. This is the run primitive recommended for agentic loops: a diverging or failing trial is reported as "success": false with the offending unit named, instead of crashing the agent's control loop with an exception.

      The dirty flag is cleared whether or not the run succeeded, so a subsequent runIfDirty() will not re-run a known-failing configuration.

      Returns:
      schema-versioned run-status JSON, with success, failedUnitName and failedUnitError fields

    • runUntilConvergedJson

      public String runUntilConvergedJson(int maxIterations, double tolerance)
      Runs the process until convergence (or an iteration limit) and returns a unified JSON report suitable for per-trial feasibility gating in agentic optimization. Never throws on a solver failure.

      For a multi-area ProcessModel, this delegates to ProcessModel.runUntilConverged(int, double) and merges ProcessModel.getConvergenceReportJson() with the run status. For a single ProcessSystem, a normal SimulationInterface.run() already iterates internal recycles; the converged flag then reflects whether the run completed without a failed unit (the RunStatus success flag).

      Top-level fields: schemaVersion, converged, runSucceeded, failedUnitName, failedUnitError, iterations, maxIterations, maxError, and (multi-area only) the nested convergence report and areas array.

      Parameters:
      maxIterations - maximum outer iterations (multi-area only); must be at least 1
      tolerance - relative convergence tolerance (multi-area only); must be finite and positive
      Returns:
      schema-versioned convergence/run JSON
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxIterations < 1 or tolerance is not a finite positive number

    • evaluate

      public String evaluate(Map<String,Double> setpoints, String setpointUnit, List<String> readbacks, String readbackUnit, int maxIterations, double tolerance)
      Atomic evaluate step for closed-loop agentic optimization: applies a batch of decision variables, runs the process until convergence, gates the result on the run status, and reads back the requested objective / constraint variables — all returned as a single JSON document. This method never throws; an infeasible trial (rejected setpoint, diverged solver, or failed unit) is reported as "feasible": false so the optimizer can penalise it instead of crashing.

      This is the recommended one-call primitive for SQP / particle-swarm / grid sweeps over a flowsheet. It removes the boilerplate of set → run → try/catch → read and avoids the common jpype pitfall of mixing java.lang.String JSON with Python parsing, because the entire trial outcome is returned as one ready-to-parse string.

      Returned fields:

      • schemaVersion — stable schema version
      • feasible — true only when every setpoint was applied, the run did not fail, and the model converged
      • converged, runSucceeded, failedUnitName, failedUnitError, iterations, maxError
      • setpointsApplied — map of address → value that were set
      • setpointsRejected — map of address → rejection reason
      • readbacks — map of address → value for successfully read outputs
      • readbackErrors — map of address → reason for unreadable outputs
      Parameters:
      setpoints - ordered map of decision-variable address → value to apply; may be null or empty to evaluate the current configuration
      setpointUnit - unit applied to every setpoint, or null for default units
      readbacks - objective / constraint addresses to read after the run; may be null or empty
      readbackUnit - unit applied to every read-back, or null for default units
      maxIterations - maximum outer iterations for the convergence run; must be at least 1
      tolerance - relative convergence tolerance; must be finite and positive
      Returns:
      schema-versioned JSON describing the trial outcome
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxIterations < 1 or tolerance is not a finite positive number

    • evaluate

      public String evaluate(Map<String,Double> setpoints, String unitOfMeasure, List<String> readbacks)
      Convenience overload of evaluate(Map, String, List, String, int, double) that uses the same unit for setpoints and read-backs and sensible default convergence settings (30 iterations, relative tolerance 5e-3, which is robust for plants with near-zero-flow anti-surge recycles).
      Parameters:
      setpoints - decision-variable address → value map; may be null or empty
      unitOfMeasure - unit applied to setpoints and read-backs, or null for default units
      readbacks - objective / constraint addresses to read after the run; may be null or empty
      Returns:
      schema-versioned JSON describing the trial outcome

    • mergeRunStatus

      private void mergeRunStatus(com.google.gson.JsonObject root)
      Merges the most recent RunStatus fields (success, failedUnitName, failedUnitError) into the supplied JSON object. Used by runUntilConvergedJson(int, double) and evaluate(Map, String, List, String, int, double).
      Parameters:
      root - the JSON object to enrich with run-status fields

    • getValues

      public Map<String,Double> getValues(List<String> addresses, String unitOfMeasure)
      Reads many variables in a single call. Reduces round-trip latency for MCP/HTTP agents that otherwise issue one call per variable.

      Each value is read in the requested unit if unitOfMeasure is non-null, otherwise in the variable's default unit. Addresses that fail to resolve are skipped and recorded in the diagnostics; the returned map only contains successfully read entries. To detect failures, compare addresses.size() to the returned map size and consult getDiagnostics().

      Parameters:
      addresses - dot-notation addresses to read
      unitOfMeasure - unit applied to every address, or null for defaults
      Returns:
      ordered map from address to value (Linked) with only successfully read entries
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if addresses is null

    • setValues

      public int setValues(Map<String,Double> updates, String unitOfMeasure, boolean runAfter)
      Sets many input variables in a single call.
      Parameters:
      updates - ordered map from address to value
      unitOfMeasure - unit applied to every update, or null for defaults
      runAfter - when true, calls run() once after all writes succeed; when false, leaves the facade dirty
      Returns:
      number of variables that were successfully set
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if updates is null

    • getWriteValidatorRegistry

      public WriteValidatorRegistry getWriteValidatorRegistry()
      Returns the registry of typed write validators consulted by setVariableValueValidated(String, double, String) and setValuesTransactional(Map, String). Defaults to WriteValidatorRegistry.createDefault(); replace it via setWriteValidatorRegistry(WriteValidatorRegistry) to disable validation or to add project-specific checks.
      Returns:
      the current registry; never null

    • setWriteValidatorRegistry

      public void setWriteValidatorRegistry(WriteValidatorRegistry registry)
      Replaces the registry of typed write validators.
      Parameters:
      registry - the new registry; must not be null

    • tryResolveEquipment

      private ProcessEquipmentInterface tryResolveEquipment(String address)
      Resolves the ProcessEquipmentInterface addressed by an automation address (handling the optional area prefix and stripping any port/property suffix). Returns null when the address cannot be resolved; never throws.
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      Returns:
      the equipment, or null when unresolvable

    • extractPropertyPath

      private String extractPropertyPath(String address)
      Returns the property path that the typed validators see (the portion of the address after the unit name). For a two-part address "Compressor.outletPressure" this is "outletPressure"; for a three-part address "Sep1.gasOut.pressure" this is "gasOut.pressure".
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      Returns:
      the property path, or null when the address has no property part

    • tryReadValue

      private Double tryReadValue(String address, String unitOfMeasure)
      Reads the current value of an address without throwing. Returns null when the value is unreadable for any reason (typically because the variable is INPUT-only and has not yet been set, or because the equipment has not been run).
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      unitOfMeasure - the unit of measure, or null for the default
      Returns:
      the current value, or null when unreadable

    • setVariableValueValidated

      public WriteValidationResult setVariableValueValidated(String address, double value, String unitOfMeasure)
      Like setVariableValue(String, double, String) but first runs the WriteValidatorRegistry against the proposed write. When validation returns ERROR the write is rejected with an IllegalArgumentException and the simulation is left unchanged.
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      value - the value to set
      unitOfMeasure - the unit of measure, or null for the default
      Returns:
      the validation result (always non-null; OK or WARNING)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if validation fails or the address cannot be resolved

    • setValuesTransactional

      public TransactionalBatchResult setValuesTransactional(Map<String,Double> updates, String unitOfMeasure)
      Applies many input writes atomically: validates every write up-front, snapshots the current values, applies the writes, runs the simulation, and rolls back to the snapshot when any phase fails.

      Rollback semantics:

      • Validation failure — if any validator returns ERROR, the batch is rejected without touching the simulation and TransactionalBatchResult.getRollbackCategory() returns VALIDATION_FAILED.
      • Apply failure — if a write throws when applied, the snapshot is restored and the simulation is re-run to coherence; APPLY_FAILED is returned.
      • Run failure — if the writes apply cleanly but run() throws, the snapshot is restored and re-run; RUN_FAILED is returned.

      Previous values that cannot be read (typically because the variable is INPUT-only and was not previously set) are recorded as null in the per-write outcome and skipped during rollback. For best results, ensure inputs have been set at least once before relying on transactional rollback.

      Parameters:
      updates - ordered map from address to value
      unitOfMeasure - unit applied to every update, or null for defaults
      Returns:
      the TransactionalBatchResult; never null
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if updates is null

    • rollbackSnapshot

      private void rollbackSnapshot(List<TransactionalBatchResult.WriteOutcome> snapshot, String unitOfMeasure)
      Restores values from a snapshot stored in TransactionalBatchResult.WriteOutcome entries and re-runs the simulation to leave it in a coherent state. Values whose snapshot is null are skipped. Restore-time errors are logged through the diagnostics but never thrown.
      Parameters:
      snapshot - the snapshot entries from the failed batch
      unitOfMeasure - the unit of measure used for the original writes

    • snapshot

      public String snapshot(String scope)
      Returns a JSON snapshot of all variables for a unit, an area, or the entire process. Useful for agent observation and for building model-vs-plant comparisons.
      Parameters:
      scope - unit name (e.g. "HP Sep"), area-qualified unit name (e.g. "Separation::HP Sep"), area name (e.g. "Separation"), or "*" / null for the whole process
      Returns:
      JSON string {schemaVersion, scope, units:[{name, area, type, variables:{...}}]}

    • getUtilizationSnapshot

      public String getUtilizationSnapshot()
      Returns a stable, side-effect-free JSON utilization snapshot of every unit in the flowsheet.

      This is the recommended observation endpoint for machine-learning / reinforcement-learning optimization loops. Whereas snapshot(String) reports raw process variables, this method reports capacity utilization — for every unit the maximum constraint utilization, the limiting constraint, a per-constraint breakdown, feasibility, and (for compressors and pumps) shaft power, plus the plant-wide bottleneck and anyOverloaded flags. Pair it with evaluate(...) (action + reward) to close an optimization loop: evaluate applies setpoints and runs the model; getUtilizationSnapshot reads back the resulting capacity observation.

      The method does not run the model; call evaluate(java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Double>, java.lang.String, java.util.List<java.lang.String>, java.lang.String, int, double) or run() first so the reported utilization reflects the latest setpoints. For a multi-area model each unit entry carries an "area" property.

      Returns:
      JSON string {schemaVersion, units:[...], bottleneck:{...}, anyOverloaded, anyHardLimitExceeded}

    • resolveScope

      private List<String> resolveScope(String scope)
      Resolves a snapshot scope string to a list of (possibly area-qualified) unit names.
      Parameters:
      scope - unit, area, area::unit, "*", or null
      Returns:
      list of unit addresses to include

    • stripUnitPrefix

      private String stripUnitPrefix(String address, String unitAddr)
      Removes a unit-name prefix from a variable address, leaving the property path.
      Parameters:
      address - full variable address
      unitAddr - unit prefix (possibly area-qualified)
      Returns:
      the property portion of the address

    • describe

      public String describe()
      Returns a single JSON manifest describing the entire flowsheet schema: areas (if any), units with type and variables, and stable schema version. This is the recommended single-tool-call discovery endpoint for LLM agents.
      Returns:
      JSON string with schema, areas, units, and variable descriptors

    • getTopology

      public String getTopology()
      Returns a JSON description of the flowsheet topology: equipment with their declared inlet and outlet streams, and explicit ProcessConnection edges when available.
      Returns:
      JSON string {schemaVersion, equipment:[{name, type, inlets, outlets}], connections:[{source, target, type, label}]}

    • getNeighbors

      public String getNeighbors(String unitName)
      Returns the upstream and downstream neighbors of a unit, derived from its inlet and outlet streams. Useful for multi-hop agent reasoning ("what feeds the HP separator?").
      Parameters:
      unitName - unit name (or area-qualified unit name in multi-area mode)
      Returns:
      JSON string {unit, upstream:[...], downstream:[...]}
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the unit cannot be resolved

    • getStructured

      public com.google.gson.JsonElement getStructured(String address)
      Returns a structured JSON element for a variable address. Unlike getVariableValue(String, String) which is scalar-only, this method supports vector and object-valued variables such as stream compositions, per-phase properties, and K-values.

      Supported address suffixes (case-insensitive):

      • <unit>.<stream>.composition → object {component: moleFraction}
      • <unit>.<stream>.molarComposition → same as composition
      • <unit>.<stream>.massComposition → object {component: massFraction}
      • <unit>.<stream>.components → array of component names
      • <unit>.<stream>.phaseFractions → object {gas, oil, aqueous} mole fractions
      • <unit>.<stream>.kvalues → object {component: K}

      Any address not matching one of these patterns is delegated to getVariableValue(String, String) and wrapped as a JSON number.

      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      Returns:
      Gson JsonElement (object, array, or primitive number)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the address cannot be resolved

    • compositionJson

      private com.google.gson.JsonObject compositionJson(SystemInterface fluid, boolean mass)
      Builds a {component → fraction} JSON object for a fluid.
      Parameters:
      fluid - the thermo system
      mass - if true returns mass fractions, otherwise mole fractions (overall)
      Returns:
      JSON object

    • validateAddress

      public AutomationDiagnostics.DiagnosticResult validateAddress(String address)
      Validates an address without throwing. Returns null if the address resolves, or a diagnostic describing why it does not.
      Parameters:
      address - the dot-notation address
      Returns:
      diagnostic on failure, null on success

    • getAllowedUnits

      public List<String> getAllowedUnits(String address)
      Returns a list of unit-of-measure strings that are valid for a given address. For now this uses the variable's unit family to suggest typical UOMs; agents can also pass null to use the variable's default unit.
      Parameters:
      address - dot-notation address
      Returns:
      ordered list of suggested UOM strings (may be empty if the unit family is unknown)
